articles.title: Karan Johar’s Double Move Shocks Fans, KWK Comeback Hides Bigger Plan
articles.description: Karan Johar is setting up a major 2026 with two big moves that have instantly grabbed attention. Koffee With Karan is returning with Season 9 as a Diwali release, promising a faster, sharper format designed for today’s audience. At the same time, he’s revisiting his controversial film Kabhi Alvida Naa Kehna—this time as a long-format web series, diving deeper into complex relationships and emotional conflict. With one project focused on real-life celebrity drama and the other on layered storytelling, Karan is clearly playing both sides—bringing back nostalgia while also reworking it for a new generation.

articles.title: Taking Ibuprofen in the Heat? Expert Warns It Could Quietly Damage Your Kidneys
articles.description: Heat, dehydration, and NSAIDs like Ibuprofen can reduce kidney blood flow, increasing the risk of acute kidney injury, especially in vulnerable individuals.

articles.title: Bomb attack kills 14, injures 38 in Colombia weeks before presidential election
articles.description: A deadly highway bomb attack in Colombia's Cauca region has killed 14 and injured 38. The blast occurred as assailants blocked the road. Authorities blame FARC dissidents. This violence escalates security concerns just weeks before the presidential election. The attack follows a wave of incidents targeting public infrastructure across southwestern Colombia.

articles.title: Trump’s First Address After Chilling ‘Assassination Attempt’ At Washington Hilton
articles.description: In his first major reveal after the shocking incident at the Washington Hilton, Donald Trump addressed the nation with a tense and measured tone. He described the attacker as a “lone wolf,” but stressed that nothing is confirmed as investigators continue examining every possible angle. The statement, delivered in the aftermath of an alleged assassination attempt, carried a heavy sense of uncertainty and urgency. While authorities piece together the motive and connections, the political atmosphere remains charged, with security fears and unanswered questions hanging over Washington.

articles.title: Trump Shooting: Did Cole Tomas Allen Create Atomic Fighting Game 'Bohrdom' On Steam? What We Know
articles.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, from California, was identified as the suspect in the White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting.

articles.title: No peace talks under shadow of blockades and threats: Iran's President Pezeshkian to Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif
articles.description: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has refused direct talks with the United States. He stated Iran will not negotiate under pressure or threats. The ongoing naval blockade and hostile US actions are the main obstacles. Pezeshkian urged the US to remove these barriers first. This comes amid stalled peace talks and a US naval blockade.

articles.title: 103, then a scare: How serious is Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s Injury? RR coach provides update
articles.description: Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi suffered an injury scare after a record-breaking century against Sunrisers Hyderabad. The 15-year-old limped off with a suspected hamstring issue, though batting coach Vikram Rathour indicated it was likely severe cramps and not serious. Sooryavanshi had earlier smashed 103 off 37 balls.

articles.description: Shots fired. Guests ducking for cover. Secret Service with guns drawn. Washington Times correspondent John T. Seward was inside the ballroom when chaos erupted at the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

articles.title: US-Iran war: Trump says Tehran presented new offers 10 minutes after he cancelled US team's trip
articles.description: President Donald Trump has rejected a new peace proposal from Iran. He stated the offer was not good enough. Trump emphasized that Iran will not possess nuclear weapons. He mentioned that negotiations were halted due to extensive travel and the absence of direct talks with Iran's leader. The US will now communicate via telephone.

articles.title: "Shadow fleet" intercepted: US Navy forces sanctioned tanker Sevan to retreat toward Iran
articles.description: The US Navy has intercepted an Iranian vessel, the M/V Sevan, in the Arabian Sea. This ship is part of Iran's 'shadow fleet' and was forced to return to Iranian waters. This action is part of a broader US naval blockade targeting Iranian energy exports. US forces are enforcing sanctions and have redirected 37 vessels since the blockade began.

articles.description: AAP's Punjab unit is regrouping after seven Rajya Sabha members defected to BJP, stressing the need for better communication ahead of assembly polls.

articles.title: Bulandshahr birthday party turns tragic, 3 shot dead over cake
articles.description: A birthday celebration in Bulandshahr, UP, ended in tragedy after an argument over smearing cake on the birthday boy's face escalated. The accused, Jeetu Saini, and his accomplices allegedly shot and killed three men. Saini is currently at large.

articles.title: Colombia: 7 killed, over 20 injured in deadly bus bombing in Cauca
articles.description: At least 7 people were killed and over 20 seriously injured after a bomb exploded on a bus in Cajibio, Colombia. Cauca Governor Octavio Guzman condemned the 'indiscriminate attack against the civilian population' on the Pan-American Highway.

articles.title: “Disrespect from the start”: Marco Trungelliti accuses Madrid Open crowd of abuse as officials face backlash over inaction
articles.description: Marco Trungelliti has accused the Madrid Open crowd of racist abuse and disrespect during his match against Daniel Merida Aguilar. The Argentine said officials did little to stop the behavior. His comments have added to the tournament’s growing list of controversies, including past complaints from Fabio Fognini, Coco Gauff, and Iga Swiatek.

An alliance with the BJP is also being considered.

articles.description: A fire broke out near Pullepady bridge in Kochi’s Ernakulam district at a waste dumping site. Fire force teams are on the spot working to control the blaze, while the cause remains unknown.
